IsA: It is an historical Castilian tables game.
HasA: It has an asymmetrical starting position.
It has three of the four quadrants used.
It has pieces that may not be 'hit'.
It has a board of 24 points.
It has 30 pieces or 'men' of two different colours.
It has two dice.
It has two dice cups.
It has a first quadrant at the bottom right.
It has a third quadrant.
It has a rail that counts as an additional point.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by an asymmetrical starting position.
It is characterized by the ludeme that an isolated hostile man may not be 'hit'.
It is characterized by the aim to move all one's men to the third quadrant and be first to bear them all off.
It is characterized by anti-clockwise movement of men.
It is characterized by white always leading.
It is characterized by dice validity rules.
It is characterized by rules for moving men.
It is characterized by rules for bearing off men.
UsedFor: It is used for playing a game.
It is used for moving men to the third quadrant.
It is used for bearing off men from the board.
LocatedIn: It is located in the first quadrant at the bottom right.
It is located in the third quadrant.
PublishedIn: It was published in the Libro de los Juegos.
Affects: It affects the way pieces are moved.
It affects the bearing off process.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a tables board of 24 points.
It consists of 30 pieces or 'men' of two different colours.
It consists of two dice cups.
PartOf: It is part of the tables game family.
It is part of the history of games.
MemberOf: It is a member of the tables game family.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the category of historical Castilian games.
KnownFor: It is known for its asymmetrical starting position.
It is known for the rule that an isolated hostile man may not be 'hit'.
It is known for being described as a new game in the 13th century.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Libro de los Juegos.
It is associated with King Alfonso of Castile.
It is associated with tables games.
About: It is about a Castilian tables game.
